classes.name: kuscia.deploy.runtime.Runtime

methods.name: kuscia.deploy.runtime.Runtime.__init__
documentation:
init runtime
self.install_path: kuscia install path
self.docker_client: docker client
self.kuscia_image: default kuscia image
self.app_image: default app image
self.network: docker network
self.protocol: NOTLS/MTLS/TLS
self.expose_port: docker container expose port?
self.skip_exists_instance: if container exists skip it
self.keep_container_volume: remove container don't remove volume
self.agent_runtime: runc/runp/runk
self.instances: all instances bind with this runtime

methods.name: kuscia.deploy.runtime.Runtime.cache_path
documentation:
get cache path
make sure self.install_path is setted before.
make_sure_exists: if cache path is not exists, will auto create

methods.name: kuscia.deploy.runtime.Runtime.bind
documentation:
bind instances with this runtime.
instance can use runtime config and canbe managered by runtime(run/stop/remove)

Kuscia Deploy Runtime Env.
Used to hold some configs and init deploy env
Every kuscia-instance(docker/pod) must bind to runtime

functions.name: kuscia.deploy.dockerhelper.copy_image_to_container
documentation:
Copy an image into a container and import it to kuscia containerd
flow: image --> docker save --> copy to container --> ctr import
image temp tar will write to: {cache_path}/{image_id}.tar.gz

classes.name: kuscia.deploy.container.docker_container.KusciaContainer

methods.name: kuscia.deploy.container.docker_container.KusciaContainer.get_public_host
documentation:
get public host name
current is only support container name

methods.name: kuscia.deploy.container.docker_container.KusciaContainer.is_started
documentation:
check container is started.
return:
    True: started success
    False: started failed
    None: starting

methods.name: kuscia.deploy.container.docker_container.KusciaContainer.run
documentation: docker run -d --name={self.container_name} [-v/-p] {image} bin/kuscia start -c etc/conf/kuscia.yaml

methods.name: kuscia.deploy.container.docker_container.KusciaContainer.stop
documentation: docker stop {self.container_name}

methods.name: kuscia.deploy.container.docker_container.KusciaContainer.remove
documentation: docker rm -f {self.container_name}

methods.name: kuscia.deploy.container.docker_container.KusciaContainer.get_file_content
documentation:
Get a file content from current docker instance
equal: docker exec {container_name} cat {path_in_container}

methods.name: kuscia.deploy.container.docker_container.KusciaContainer.put_files
documentation:
Copy files into container
local_prefix}/{file} ---> path_in_container/{file}

methods.name: kuscia.deploy.container.docker_container.KusciaContainer.has_master
documentation: Current Instance has k3s master?

methods.name: kuscia.deploy.container.docker_container.KusciaContainer.import_image
documentation:
Copy docker image (in local machine) into current container
Used for lite/autonomy to import app image

methods.name: kuscia.deploy.container.docker_container.KusciaContainer.exec
documentation: Exec a command in current container, and return std/stderr output string

Kuscia Instance using docker
every instance is a docker container
self.ports: port mappings. it is a readonly value
self.container_config: container input configs
    self.container_config['ports']: container need mapping ports to local
    self.container_config['volumes']: container need mapping volumes to local

classes.name: kuscia.deploy.instance.KusciaInstance

methods.name: kuscia.deploy.instance.KusciaInstance.__init__
documentation:
self.mode: Master/Lite/Autonomy
self.private_key: domain's private key, will auto create when call self.get_config_file
self.config: kuscia configs (content will write to kuscia.yaml)
             self.config['domainKeyData'] will auto overwrite by self.private_key

methods.name: kuscia.deploy.instance.KusciaInstance.get_config_file
documentation:
create a config file using self.config
return config file path: {install_path}/{domain_id}.yaml

KusciaInstance maybe is docker or pod...
A Kuscia domain is comprised of at least one KusciaInstance.
KusciaInstance used to manager kuscia config
